entries.management_implication: Use this plan as active statewide policy baseline.
entries.model_use_guidance: Policy reference only; not direct p_draw input.
entries.evidence_excerpt: ...in effect for 6 years (December 2024 through December 2030).

entries.management_implication: Anchor for unit plan rollups and statewide progress tracking.
entries.evidence_excerpt: The current statewide population objective ... is 404,900.

entries.management_implication: Confirms statewide herd below objective.
entries.evidence_excerpt: 2023 postseason estimate approximately 279,000 deer; 69% of objective.

entries.management_implication: General units managed to one of these bands.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Manage general-season units for 15-17 or 18-20 bucks per 100 does.

entries.management_implication: LE units target tighter mature buck structure.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Manage designated limited-entry units for 25-30 bucks per 100 does.

entries.management_implication: Premium LE top-end quality bracket.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Manage premium limited-entry units for 40-45 bucks per 100 does.

entries.management_implication: Changes up to plus/minus 20% can be automatic.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Changes over 20% go through RAC and Wildlife Board process.

entries.management_implication: Baseline seasonal permit allocation guidance.
entries.evidence_excerpt: 20% archery, 20% muzzleloader, 60% any weapon.

entries.metric: Split When Early Any Weapon Added
entries.management_implication: Alternative split when early any hunt used.
entries.evidence_excerpt: ...20/20/20/40 allocation when early any legal weapon hunt is added.

entries.management_implication: Small share can be converted to multi-season permits.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Allow 3% of hunters to hunt all seasons for increased fee.

entries.value: up to 0.5% with minimum 5 permits per unit
entries.management_implication: Bounded additional LE late-season opportunity.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Up to 0.5% of general-season draw total with minimum of 5 permits each unit.

entries.management_implication: Premium LE includes management-buck carveout.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Between 10-20% of premium LE permits issued as management buck permits.

entries.management_implication: Current-cycle supply benchmark.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Total general-season deer permits available in 2024 was 71,525.

entries.management_implication: Harvest throughput anchor.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Average of 25,062 bucks harvested each year over last 10 years.

entries.management_implication: Recent success benchmark.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Statewide average hunter success in 2023 was 35.0%.

entries.management_implication: Adult doe survival strongly influences growth.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Adult female survival averaged 79.8%.

entries.management_implication: Recruitment variability is significant.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Fawn survival averaged 52.1%.

entries.management_implication: Survey design floor for composition estimates.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Classify a minimum of 400 does on each unit.

entries.management_implication: Habitat target tied to population objective.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Improve at least 600,000 acres of crucial range by 2030.

entries.metric: Resident odds drawing LE buck permit
entries.management_implication: Demand pressure materially higher than historical baseline.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Resident odds of drawing limited-entry buck permit were 1 in 25.4 in 2024.

entries.management_implication: Annual-access probability lower than historical baseline.
entries.evidence_excerpt: General-season permit odds moved from 1 in 1.1 (2000) to 1 in 2.3 (2024).

entries.value: detection of first positive (about 1% prevalence surveillance target)
entries.management_implication: Do not wait for high prevalence before management changes in affected units.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Threshold for CWD management actions in Utah should be set at detection of CWD.

entries.metric: Sample size for at least 1% prevalence detection at 95% confidence
entries.management_implication: Surveillance adequacy benchmark.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Required sample size for 1% prevalence at 95% confidence is 304 deer and 346 elk.

entries.value: Use age metrics for quality context only; not direct permit quota or p_draw overrides
entries.management_implication: Preserves separation between quality features and draw mechanics.
entries.model_use_guidance: Non-causal draw input unless explicitly validated.
entries.evidence_excerpt: Implementation guardrail for HUNT-BUILDER from plan intent and governance rules.
